body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: shims-are-listeners dispatch infrastructure
body.description:
**What changed:** CCWorkerShim now runs the dispatch listener as a background thread inside a rack supervisor process, replacing subprocess-based cc_worker_listener. Web UI controls circuit breaker state. Granny wakes on ticket insert.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Blast radius concentration: previously each cc_worker_listener subprocess was independent; now one rack_supervisor crash takes down ALL device listener threads simultaneously. A crash that would have affected one slot now affects all. Watch for cascade silent failures.
- Thread leak: if stop() fails to join the listener thread (timeout exceeded), the thread becomes orphaned and consumes IMAP connections indefinitely. Check for zombie threads under load.
- ensure_daemon_running() bidirectional: the new OPEN+alive→stop() path is new behavior. If called during an active handshake (ticket mid-ack), stop() will cancel it. Watch for tickets stuck in 'assigned' with no ack_note.
- Granny LISTEN/NOTIFY: if the DB connection that holds the LISTEN drops (network hiccup), Granny goes silent until the next 60s fallback poll fires. Could appear as a missed dispatch. Watch for tickets staying 'sprint' without GRANNY_DISPATCH log entry.
- Circuit state defaults: if circuit_state.json is absent (fresh deploy), all slots default to CLOSED. If DS.0's shim doesn't exist yet, the supervisor will fail to instantiate it and may crash or skip it silently.

**Signals to watch:**
- `ps aux | grep rack_supervisor` should show exactly one process
- No cc_worker_listener processes in ps aux
- Granny channel: GRANNY_DISPATCH entries should resume within 5s of ticket insert (not 60s)
- No tickets stuck in 'assigned' longer than 15 minutes without an ack note
- Web UI device card shows correct on/off state after toggle (manual smoke test)

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-07-02 or when T-web-device-controls is closed, whichever comes first.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
